perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until terminated in accordance with this Section 1. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time with or without cause by giving ten (10) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue indefinitely. Either party may terminate this Agreement only with mutual written consent from both parties, and under no circumstances may either party unilaterally terminate the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows either party to terminate the agreement with ten days' notice. The changed text states that the agreement continues indefinitely and can only be terminated with mutual consent. This directly contradicts the original termination clause, creating uncertainty about the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated. This is further contradicted in section 10, which implies ways in which the document can be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and will not be copied or reproduced without the express written permission of the Disclosing Party, except for copies that are necessary in order to fulfill the Purpose. Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed, except that the Receiving Party may retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information, to be used only in case of a dispute concerning this Agreement. The return of any Confidential Information will not relieve the Receiving Party of its obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information for the period provided for in Section 1 above. Section 6 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement continues indefinitely and can only be terminated with mutual consent. This directly contradicts the original termination clause, creating uncertainty about the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated. The contradicted text states that "Section 6 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement," but the changed text states that the agreement may never terminate. Therefore, the survival clause in the contradicted text becomes meaningless, thus resulting in a direct contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' type, as the modification introduces a clear conflict regarding the termination conditions.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information for any purpose deemed beneficial by the Receiving Party, irrespective of the Purpose outlined herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly limits the use of Confidential Information to the stated 'Purpose'. The changed text removes this restriction and allows the Receiving Party to use the information for any purpose they see fit, directly contradicting the original limitation. This creates contradiction with the section that describes the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, both Company and AST (collectively, the “Parties” and, individually, a “Party”) desire to exchange with each other information relating to potential patent acquisition opportunities for the purpose of AST evaluating and pursuing such opportunities (the “Purpose”) and for no other purpose
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text limits the use of Confidential Information to the 'Purpose', while the changed text allows its use for any purpose deemed beneficial by the Receiving Party. The contradicted text explicitly states the 'Purpose' and limits information exchange to 'no other purpose'. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction by removing the 'Purpose' limitation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will impose no obligation upon the Receiving Party with respect to any portion of the received Confidential Information which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of receiving such Confidential Information or is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; (b) is in the public domain prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party, or subsequently enters the public domain without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party, without restriction, from a source other than the Disclosing Party or any of its employees or directors, without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (d) is hereafter furnished to the Receiving Party by a third party without breach by such third party of an obligation of confidentiality of which the Receiving Party is actually aware; or (e) is permitted to be disclosed by the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is obligated to treat all information received as Confidential Information, regardless of whether it (a) was already known to the Receiving Party, (b) is in the public domain, (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party from another source, (d) is furnished to the Receiving Party by a third party, or (e) is permitted to be disclosed by prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text lists exceptions to the Receiving Party's obligations regarding Confidential Information. The changed text eliminates these exceptions, stating that all information received must be treated as Confidential Information regardless of its source or prior knowledge. This creates a direct conflict with the enumerated exceptions, leading to uncertainty about the scope of the confidentiality obligations. By making the statement that regardless of the cases established here are neglected, then the Receiving party is obliged to protect the data regardless of what is stated in section 3 a-e.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will impose no obligation upon the Receiving Party with respect to any portion of the received Confidential Information which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of receiving such Confidential Information or is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; (b) is in the public domain prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party, or subsequently enters the public domain without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party, without restriction, from a source other than the Disclosing Party or any of its employees or directors, without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (d) is hereafter furnished to the Receiving Party by a third party without breach by such third party of an obligation of confidentiality of which the Receiving Party is actually aware; or (e) is permitted to be disclosed by the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct conflict. The original text explicitly lists exceptions to the Receiving Party's obligation to treat information as confidential. The changed text eliminates these exceptions, stating that ALL information must be treated as confidential. This contradicts the explicitly stated exceptions in the 'Contradicted Text' section, which is also where the 'Original Text' was located, fulfilling the criteria for an in-text contradiction under 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as the clause is placed in the same location with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required to be disclosed by applicable law, regulation or an order of a governmental agency, legislative body or court of competent jurisdiction; provided that the Receiving Party provides the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement, so that the Disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order and/or waive compliance with this Agreement. The Receiving Party will cooperate with the Disclosing Party (at the Disclosing Party’s sole expense) in order that the Disclosing Party may obtain a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall not, under any circumstances, disclose Confidential Information, even if required by applicable law, regulation, or an order of a governmental agency, legislative body, or court of competent jurisdiction. The Receiving Party is solely responsible for challenging any such legal demands and protecting the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows the Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information if legally required, provided they notify the Disclosing Party. The changed text prohibits disclosure under any circumstances, contradicting the original provision. This creates a significant conflict about the Receiving Party's obligations in the face of legal demands. This is further making a contradiction with what is established in section 3 where some disclosures of confidential information are allowed, but here that is being denied under any legal body.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will impose no obligation upon the Receiving Party with respect to any portion of the received Confidential Information which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of receiving such Confidential Information or is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; (b) is in the public domain prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party, or subsequently enters the public domain without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party, without restriction, from a source other than the Disclosing Party or any of its employees or directors, without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (d) is hereafter furnished to the Receiving Party by a third party without breach by such third party of an obligation of confidentiality of which the Receiving Party is actually aware; or (e) is permitted to be disclosed by the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text imposes an absolute prohibition on disclosing confidential information, even when legally required. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which lists circumstances under which the Receiving Party has no obligation to maintain confidentiality, including situations where disclosure is permitted with the Disclosing Party's consent. The original text allowed for legally required disclosure with notification to the Disclosing Party. The changed text makes such disclosure impossible, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party in accordance with this Agreement. However, disclosure by the Disclosing Party of Confidential Information does not constitute a warranty that the Confidential Information is accurate, complete, or adequate for the purposes contemplated by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party in accordance with this Agreement. Furthermore, the Disclosing Party guarantees that all Confidential Information is accurate, complete, and adequate for the purposes contemplated by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that while the Disclosing Party has the right to disclose information, it does not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of that information. The changed text contradicts this by guaranteeing the accuracy, completeness, and adequacy of the information. This creates a conflict regarding the Disclosing Party's responsibility for the quality of the Confidential Information. In this part the Disclosing party claims that has the right to disclose confidental information to the Receiving party, however, at the end it says that they guarantee that the all confidential information is accurate, complete, and adequate for the purposes contemplated by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Disclosing Party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party in accordance with this Agreement. However, disclosure by the Disclosing Party of Confidential Information does not constitute a warranty that the Confidential Information is accurate, complete, or adequate for the purposes contemplated by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Disclosing Party does not warrant the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the Confidential Information. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the Disclosing Party guarantees the accuracy, completeness, and adequacy of the Confidential Information. The contradicted text is the same as the original text, therefore this change causes a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed, except that the Receiving Party may retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information, to be used only in case of a dispute concerning this Agreement. The return of any Confidential Information will not relieve the Receiving Party of its obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information for the period provided for in Section 1 above. Section 6 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Regardless of any written demand by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party is permanently entitled to possess and use all Confidential Information without any obligation to return or destroy it. The Receiving Party is under no obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information after receiving a written demand from the Disclosing Party. Section 6 shall not survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Receiving Party to return or destroy Confidential Information upon written demand, but allows them to keep an archival copy. It also states that the obligation to maintain confidentiality survives the return of the information. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the Receiving Party is permanently entitled to possess and use the information, is not obligated to return or destroy it, and is not obligated to maintain confidentiality after a written demand. This creates a complete reversal of the Receiving Party's obligations regarding the Confidential Information after a demand. Also, it is directly contradicting the original statements where the receiving party should maintain confidentiality of the Confidential Information for the period provided for in Section 1 above.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and will not be copied or reproduced without the express written permission of the Disclosing Party, except for copies that are necessary in order to fulfill the Purpose. Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed, except that the Receiving Party may retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information, to be used only in case of a dispute concerning this Agreement. The return of any Confidential Information will not relieve the Receiving Party of its obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information for the period provided for in Section 1 above. Section 6 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original and contradicted text states that the Receiving Party must return or destroy Confidential Information upon written demand, maintain confidentiality, and that Section 6 survives termination. The changed text states the Receiving Party is permanently entitled to possess and use the information, is not obligated to return or destroy it, and is not obligated to maintain confidentiality after a written demand. Also, Section 6 will not survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement. This creates a direct conflict within the document, fitting the definition of a Structural Flaw- In Text Contradiction as a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
